story.iWant: to choose which format to convert my preset to
story.soThat: I can get the output format I need for my photography software
story.tasks: - Display 3 format options (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate) with radio buttons - Show format badges with icons/colors (reuse from Story 2-3) - Each option shows: name, extension, compatible software, description - Pre-select target format based on detected source (NP3→XMP, XMP→NP3, lrtemplate→XMP) - Disable same-format conversion with tooltip - Store selected target format in application state - Display "Convert to [Format]" button - Visual feedback on selection change (highlight, animation)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-1: Format Selection UI Component - Display 3 format options after parameter preview - Use radio buttons for clarity - Show format badges with icons/colors - Each option shows: name, extension, compatible software, brief description
AC-2: Smart Default Selection - Pre-select based on source: NP3→XMP, XMP→NP3, lrtemplate→XMP - Default selected on load, user can change
AC-3: Disable Same-Format Conversion - Disable option if source = target format - Show tooltip: "Cannot convert to same format" - Visually distinguish disabled option
AC-4: Format Descriptions (Educational) - NP3: Nikon Picture Control, .np3, Nikon cameras - XMP: Lightroom CC Preset, .xmp, cloud sync - lrtemplate: Lightroom Classic Preset, .lrtemplate, legacy format
AC-5: Store Selected Format - Store targetFormat in state - Accessible to Story 2-6 - Update on selection change - Clear on new file upload
AC-6: Display Convert Button - Show after format selection - Enabled when target selected - Primary action style - Text: "Convert to [Format Name]"
AC-7: Visual Feedback on Selection Change - Highlight selected option - Smooth transition (200ms) - Button text updates immediately

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Goal: User selects output format. Deliverables: Format selection dropdown/radio buttons, Smart default (XMP→NP3, NP3→XMP), Validation (prevent same-format conversion). Complexity: Simple (0.5-1 day). Dependency: Story 2-3 (need detected format).

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Decision: Vanilla JavaScript (zero dependencies). Rationale: Epic 2 UI is simple, zero framework overhead (target: <100KB total JS), faster initial load time. State management via closures/objects.

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Support Matrix: Chrome/Firefox/Safari (Latest 2). Required: WebAssembly, File API, Drag-and-drop, Promises/async-await. IE11 not supported (no WASM).

doc.title: Story 2-3: Format Detection
doc.snippet: Provides detectFileFormat() function that returns "np3"|"xmp"|"lrtemplate". getCurrentFormat() returns cached detected format. Dispatches 'formatDetected' CustomEvent for Story 2-5 to listen.

doc.title: Story 2-4: Parameter Preview Display
doc.snippet: Displays parameters before format selection. Parameter panel appears after format detection. Shows Basic/Color/Detail adjustments. Story 2-5 format selector should appear below parameter panel.

artifact.reason: Main entry point that listens for 'formatDetected' event. Story 2-5 needs to add another listener here for format selection integration.
Returns currently detected format ("np3"|"xmp"|"lrtemplate"). Story 2-5 needs this to determine source format for smart defaults.
Maps format codes to human-readable names. Story 2-5 can reuse this for format option labels.
Returns CSS class for format badge colors (badge-blue, badge-purple, badge-teal). Story 2-5 can reuse these colors for format options.
Displays parameter panel. Story 2-5 format selector should appear after this panel in the UI flow.
Placeholder div for conversion controls. Story 2-5 format selector should be rendered inside this div.
Existing format badge styles (pill-shaped, colored). Story 2-5 can reuse these styling patterns for format options.

ecosystem.name: vanilla-javascript
ecosystem.note: Zero npm dependencies - Pure vanilla JavaScript with ES6 modules
Browser APIs used: CustomEvent, DOM manipulation, CSS Grid

constraints.constraint: - Must use vanilla JavaScript (no React, Vue, or other frameworks per Tech Spec Decision 1) - Must use ES6 module pattern (import/export) - Must follow event-driven architecture (CustomEvent for component communication) - Must use radio buttons for format selection (not dropdown, per story decision)

- Format selector must appear AFTER parameter preview panel (Story 2-4) - Must show all 3 format options (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate) even if one is disabled - Must pre-select smart default (NP3→XMP, XMP→NP3, lrtemplate→XMP) - Must disable same-format conversion (source = target) - Convert button text must update dynamically based on selected format
- Must work in Chrome, Firefox, Safari (Latest 2 versions) - Must use standard Web APIs only (no browser-specific features) - Must be responsive (desktop, tablet, mobile breakpoints)
- Target format must be stored in module-level state (not global) - State must be accessible to Story 2-6 (conversion execution) - State must clear when new file is uploaded

- Must reuse existing badge colors from Story 2-3 (badge-blue, badge-purple, badge-teal) - Must follow existing CSS patterns (pill shapes, spacing, colors) - Must use CSS Grid for layout (not flexbox or tables)

interface.name: getCurrentFormat()
interface.usage: Call this to get source format for smart default logic

interface.name: getFormatDisplayName(format)
interface.usage: Use to generate format option labels (e.g., "XMP (Lightroom CC)")

interface.name: getFormatBadgeClass(format)
interface.usage: Use to get CSS class for format option styling (badge-blue, badge-purple, badge-teal)

interface.name: formatDetected event
interface.usage: Listen for this event to trigger format selector display

interface.name: formatSelected event
interface.usage: Dispatch when user selects target format (optional for analytics/logging)

interface.name: convertRequest event
interface.usage: Dispatch when user clicks Convert button (Story 2-6 will listen)

interface.name: getTargetFormat()
interface.usage: Story 2-6 will call this to get selected target format for conversion

tests.standards: Per Epic 2 Tech Spec, testing follows manual browser testing approach: - Manual testing in Chrome, Firefox, Safari (Latest 2 versions) - Use sample files from examples/ directory - Browser DevTools for console verification - No automated E2E tests in MVP (deferred to Epic 6) - Code quality verified through code review against acceptance criteria
tests.locations: - web/index.html (UI testing - visual verification) - Browser DevTools Console (event logging, state verification) - examples/np3/, examples/lrtemplate/ (test files)

ideas.test: Upload NP3 file → verify XMP pre-selected (smart default)
Upload XMP file → verify NP3 pre-selected
Upload lrtemplate file → verify XMP pre-selected
Verify all 3 format options displayed with correct names and icons
Upload NP3 file → verify NP3 option disabled with tooltip
Verify format descriptions are accurate and educational
Call getTargetFormat() in console → verify returns correct format
Upload new file → verify target format resets to smart default
Verify Convert button visible and text matches selected format
Click different format option → verify smooth transition animation (200ms)
Verify Convert button text updates immediately on format change
Test format selector appears below parameter panel (Story 2-4)
Test formatDetected event triggers format selector display
Test desktop layout (1920px) - format options clear spacing
Test mobile layout (400px) - format header stacks vertically
